title: Aspect Markers
description: Master đã/rồi, đang, sẽ/sắp, vừa/mới — Vietnamese time markers
intro: Vietnamese doesn't conjugate verbs for tense. Instead, time words (aspect markers) go before or after the verb to show when something happens. Master these 7 markers and you can express any time frame.

questions.explanation: 'đang' = currently doing. 'Tôi đang ăn' = I am eating (right now)
'đã' = already (past action). 'Tôi đã ăn' = I already ate / I have eaten
'sẽ' = will (future). 'Tôi sẽ đi' = I will go

questions.prompt: Tôi ___ ăn cơm. (I am eating rice — right now)
questions.explanation: 'đang' for actions happening right now: I AM eating
'đã...rồi' = already did. 'đã' before verb + 'rồi' at end is the full past pattern

questions.prompt: What does 'rồi' add to a sentence?
questions.explanation: 'rồi' = already/done. Can work alone ('Ăn rồi' = Already ate) or with 'đã' ('đã ăn rồi')

questions.prompt: Tôi ___ đi Đà Nẵng. (I will go to Da Nang.)
questions.explanation: 'sẽ' before the verb for future: I WILL go
'Tôi sẽ đi' = I will go (someday). 'Tôi sắp đi' = I'm about to go (very soon)
'sắp' for imminent events: 'It's ABOUT TO rain!' — happening very soon

questions.prompt: Which TWO markers both mean 'just' (very recent past)?
questions.explanation: 'vừa' and 'mới' both = just (did something). 'Tôi vừa ăn' = 'Tôi mới ăn' = I just ate

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I haven't eaten yet'?
questions.explanation: 'chưa' = not yet. Different from 'không' (don't/won't). 'chưa ăn' = haven't eaten yet

questions.prompt: Bạn ăn ___ chưa? (Have you eaten yet?)
questions.explanation: 'Ăn cơm chưa?' is literally 'Eat rice yet?' — this is a common Vietnamese greeting, not just about food!

questions.prompt: Put the markers in time order: earliest to latest
questions.explanation: Past (đã) → Just now (vừa/mới) → Now (đang) → About to (sắp) → Future (sẽ)
